The compliance dashboard (GET /v1/compliance/dashboard) provides a unified view of system health for coordinated planning. The gateway proxy coordinates with the AgentAnchor platform for distributed contingency scenarios.
cp-2.1.remarks: Cognigate provides multi-framework health visibility for coordinated planning. Consumer organization coordinates contingency plans with related organizational plans.

cp-2.3.title: Contingency Plan | Resume Mission-Essential Functions
cp-2.3.description: Mission-essential function resumption is supported through the circuit breaker recovery mechanism. The HALF_OPEN state (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) allows limited requests (half_open_requests: 3) to test system recovery before full resumption. Manual reset via POST /v1/admin/circuit/reset enables administrator-initiated recovery. Entity-level recovery via POST /v1/admin/entity/unhalt restores individual agent operations. The health endpoint hierarchy supports orchestrated restarts with /health/live (process alive) and /ready (service ready) probes.
cp-2.3.remarks: Cognigate supports graduated recovery of mission-essential functions. Consumer organization defines mission-essential functions and recovery priorities.

cp-2.8.title: Contingency Plan | Identify Critical Assets
cp-2.8.description: Critical assets are identified through the system architecture: (1) The proof chain database (PostgreSQL) containing the immutable audit trail; (2) Ed25519 signing keys for non-repudiation; (3) Policy definitions (three default BASIS policies); (4) Configuration settings (admin API key, database URL, API keys); (5) Agent registry with trust scores and capabilities. The health endpoint (GET /health) monitors critical subsystem health (database, cache, signatures). The compliance dashboard identifies critical control health across all frameworks.
cp-2.8.remarks: Cognigate identifies and monitors its critical assets. Consumer organization maintains a comprehensive critical asset inventory including Cognigate within their system boundary.

cp-3.title: Contingency Training
cp-3.description: Contingency training support is provided through Cognigate's interactive developer tools. The playground (/tools/playground) allows administrators to test the full governance pipeline (INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF) in a safe environment. The trust calculator (/tools/calculator) enables exploration of trust tier implications during contingency scenarios. The error codes reference (/tools/errors) documents 38+ error scenarios for incident response training. The admin API documentation covers circuit breaker halt/reset, entity halt/unhalt, and velocity control procedures.
cp-3.remarks: Cognigate provides training tools for contingency procedures. Consumer organization establishes formal contingency training programs with schedule, content, and completion tracking.

cp-4.title: Contingency Plan Testing
cp-4.description: Contingency plan testing is supported through comprehensive test suites including: test_circuit_breaker.py (circuit breaker state transitions, auto-reset, entity halt/unhalt, cascade halt), test_chaos.py (chaos engineering scenarios), test_adversarial.py (adversarial attack scenarios), and test_regressions.py (regression testing). The circuit breaker's manual_trip and manual_reset functions enable controlled contingency testing via admin endpoints. The compliance monitoring trigger (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) allows on-demand compliance health verification during contingency exercises.
cp-4.remarks: Cognigate provides automated and manual testing capabilities for contingency scenarios. Consumer organization conducts formal contingency plan testing exercises.

cp-4.1.title: Contingency Plan Testing | Coordinate with Related Plans
cp-4.1.description: Coordinated contingency testing is supported through the multi-framework compliance monitoring. The compliance health endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/health) provides real-time health across 9 frameworks during testing exercises. The admin status endpoint (GET /v1/admin/status) provides comprehensive system health for coordination with infrastructure teams. The gateway health check (GET /v1/gateway/status) monitors external system availability during contingency exercises.
cp-4.1.remarks: Cognigate provides health monitoring APIs for coordinated testing. Consumer organization coordinates testing with related organizational plans.

cp-6.title: Alternate Storage Site
cp-6.description: Alternate storage is supported through the database configuration flexibility. Cognigate supports PostgreSQL (production, configured via DATABASE_URL), SQLite (development/emergency fallback), and Vercel serverless with Neon PostgreSQL for cloud deployments. The DATABASE_URL environment variable allows redirecting storage to alternate sites without code changes. Ed25519 signing keys can be loaded from file (signature_key_path), environment variable (signature_private_key in base64), or generated ephemerally, enabling key recovery at alternate sites.
cp-6.remarks: Cognigate supports flexible database configuration for alternate storage. Consumer organization establishes and maintains alternate storage sites with appropriate geographic separation.

cp-6.1.title: Alternate Storage Site | Separation from Primary Site
cp-6.1.description: Separation from primary storage is supported through the DATABASE_URL configuration which can point to geographically separated PostgreSQL instances. Neon serverless PostgreSQL provides built-in replication capabilities. The proof chain's hash-based integrity verification enables chain validation after transfer to alternate storage.
cp-6.1.remarks: Cognigate's database portability supports alternate site separation. Cloud infrastructure provider and consumer organization manage geographic separation.

cp-6.3.title: Alternate Storage Site | Accessibility
cp-6.3.description: Alternate storage accessibility is supported through Cognigate's database abstraction layer (app/db/database.py) which transparently connects to any PostgreSQL instance via DATABASE_URL. The health endpoint (GET /health) includes database connectivity checks with latency measurement, enabling automated failover detection. The readiness probe (GET /ready) indicates when the service is ready to accept requests after storage migration.
cp-6.3.remarks: Cognigate supports automated database failover detection. Consumer organization ensures alternate storage site accessibility during contingency events.

cp-7.title: Alternate Processing Site
cp-7.description: Alternate processing is supported through Cognigate's stateless architecture and containerized deployment. The Vercel deployment model provides automatic scaling across global edge locations. The application state is externalized to the database (PostgreSQL via DATABASE_URL) and optional cache (Redis via redis_url), enabling any instance to process requests. The /v1/admin/circuit/reset endpoint enables rapid recovery at alternate processing sites. The lifespan manager handles independent subsystem initialization for clean startup at new sites.
cp-7.remarks: Cognigate's stateless architecture supports deployment at alternate processing sites. Consumer organization establishes alternate processing site agreements and procedures.

cp-7.1.title: Alternate Processing Site | Separation from Primary Site
cp-7.1.description: Processing site separation is supported through Cognigate's cloud-native deployment architecture. Vercel provides multi-region deployment capabilities. The stateless application design allows deployment at geographically separated sites without data synchronization concerns beyond the database. The DATABASE_URL and redis_url configurations enable independent site-specific infrastructure.
cp-7.1.remarks: Cognigate supports multi-region deployment. Cloud infrastructure provider manages geographic separation of processing sites.

cp-7.2.title: Alternate Processing Site | Accessibility
cp-7.2.description: Alternate processing site accessibility is ensured through the health check hierarchy: /health/live (liveness probe), /ready (readiness probe), and /health (full health check with subsystem status). These endpoints support Kubernetes and serverless health monitoring for automated failover. The circuit breaker's manual_reset enables rapid recovery at alternate sites.
cp-7.2.remarks: Cognigate provides health probes for automated failover. Consumer organization ensures alternate site accessibility through network and infrastructure configuration.

cp-7.3.title: Alternate Processing Site | Priority of Service
cp-7.3.description: Priority of service at alternate sites is supported through the trust tier system which prioritizes high-trust entities. The velocity cap system (app/core/velocity.py) allocates more resources to higher-trust entities (T7: 500 actions/second vs T0: 2 actions/second), providing inherent service prioritization during capacity-constrained recovery scenarios.
cp-7.3.remarks: Trust-tier-based resource allocation provides implicit service prioritization. Consumer organization establishes formal priority of service agreements.

cp-8.title: Telecommunications Services
cp-8.description: Telecommunications services for Cognigate are provided by the cloud infrastructure platform (Vercel edge network, AWS). Cognigate operates as a cloud SaaS application and does not manage telecommunications infrastructure directly. The HTTPS API interface operates over standard internet connections provided by the cloud hosting platform.
cp-8.remarks: Telecommunications services are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider. Consumer organization ensures adequate telecommunications connectivity to the cloud service.

cp-8.1.title: Telecommunications Services | Priority of Service Provisions
cp-8.1.description: Priority of telecommunications service provisions are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider (Vercel/AWS). Cognigate does not manage telecommunications priority directly.

cp-8.2.title: Telecommunications Services | Single Points of Failure
cp-8.2.description: Single points of failure in telecommunications are managed by the cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate's stateless architecture and multi-region deployment capability reduce application-level single points of failure.
cp-8.2.remarks: Inherited from cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate's architecture minimizes application-level single points of failure.

cp-9.title: System Backup
cp-9.description: System backup is supported through the database layer. PostgreSQL instances (Neon serverless) provide automated backups. The proof chain's hash-based integrity enables backup verification through chain integrity checks (verify_chain_integrity in app/db/proof_repository.py). Configuration backup is managed through Git version control for code and environment variable management for runtime settings. Ed25519 signing keys can be exported via export_private_key_pem() for secure key backup. The chain_state table tracks the current chain position and last hash for backup consistency verification.
cp-9.remarks: Cognigate supports database backup verification and key export. Consumer organization establishes backup schedules, retention, and testing procedures.

cp-9.1.title: System Backup | Testing Reliability and Integrity
cp-9.1.description: Backup reliability testing is supported through the proof chain integrity verification (verify_chain_integrity in app/db/proof_repository.py) which validates the entire chain from genesis hash through all records, checking previous_hash linkage and chain_position sequencing. This verification can be run against backup databases to confirm integrity. The health endpoint database connectivity check verifies database accessibility and responsiveness.
cp-9.1.remarks: Cognigate provides chain integrity verification for backup validation. Consumer organization establishes backup testing schedules and procedures.

cp-9.8.title: System Backup | Cryptographic Protection
cp-9.8.description: Cryptographic protection of backups is provided at multiple levels: (1) The proof chain records are individually signed with Ed25519 digital signatures; (2) Each record includes SHA-256 hash integrity; (3) The chain linkage provides tamper-evidence across the entire backup. Database-level encryption is provided by the PostgreSQL infrastructure (Neon provides encryption at rest). Transport encryption is provided by TLS for database connections.
cp-9.8.remarks: Application-level cryptographic integrity is provided by the proof chain. Storage-level encryption is provided by the database infrastructure.

cp-10.title: System Recovery and Reconstitution
cp-10.description: System recovery is implemented through the circuit breaker recovery mechanism (CLOSED -> OPEN -> HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED) with automatic recovery testing (half_open_requests: 3 test requests before full recovery). Manual recovery via POST /v1/admin/circuit/reset enables administrator-initiated reconstitution. Entity-level recovery via POST /v1/admin/entity/unhalt restores individual agents. The lifespan manager (app/main.py) handles clean startup with independent subsystem initialization. Database reconstitution is supported through proof chain integrity verification which detects any data corruption after recovery. The health endpoint hierarchy (/health/live, /ready, /health) supports orchestrated recovery verification.
cp-10.remarks: Cognigate provides automated and manual recovery mechanisms. Consumer organization establishes recovery procedures, priorities, and acceptance criteria.

cp-10.2.title: System Recovery and Reconstitution | Transaction Recovery
cp-10.2.description: Transaction recovery is supported through the proof chain's sequential ordering (chain_position) and hash linkage (previous_hash). If the chain is interrupted, the last valid chain position can be identified through verify_chain_integrity. The chain_state table (app/db/models.py) maintains the last_hash and chain_length for recovery point identification. Database transaction integrity is managed by PostgreSQL's ACID properties. The async session management in app/db/database.py provides proper transaction handling with flush and commit semantics.
cp-10.2.remarks: Proof chain integrity and database ACID properties support transaction recovery. Consumer organization establishes transaction recovery procedures.
